title: Regime-specialist mixture of experts
summary: Official strategy guides repeatedly separate low-degradation one-stop races, high-degradation two-stop races, and wet/intermediate races.
rationale: Official strategy guides repeatedly separate low-degradation one-stop races, high-degradation two-stop races, and wet/intermediate races. A single global model can blur these distinct decision policies.
implementation_hint: Train specialist models for regimes such as dry-low-deg, dry-high-deg, wet/intermediate, and late-race finish-window states. Use a simple gating model driven by `Compound`, wet flag, race-level median degradation, estimated total laps, and race identity to blend expert outputs.
expected_effect: Higher robustness across circuit/year shifts, especially where one global set of splits underfits rare but structurally different strategy regimes.
risk: Rare regimes, especially wet, may be too small; regularize heavily and fall back to the global model when gate confidence is weak.
